Method 4: FFmpeg command-line batch (free, scriptable)
For developers and creators comfortable with the terminal, FFmpeg's lut3d filter
Single file command: ffmpeg dash i input dot mp4 dash vf lut3d equals your dot c
Batch loop in bash: for f in star dot mp4, do ffmpeg dash i quote f quote dash v
FFmpeg uses tetrahedral interpolation by default (higher quality than trilinear)

Common pitfalls
Pitfall two: cross-genre application. A LUT designed for moody indoor portraits
Pitfall three: 100 percent intensity by default. Most commercial LUTs are design
Pitfall four: forgetting white balance. If your shots have inconsistent white ba
All four methods support intensity control, but with different mechanics.
SammaPix: an Advanced collapsible reveals a zero to 100 percent intensity slider
Lightroom: the Profile Amount slider goes zero to 200, where 100 is the LUT's na

FFmpeg: no built-in intensity. Workaround: apply the LUT to a copy of the clip,
